jokes.text: I don't get why you brush your buggalo. They have no hair. - Shh. They don't know that. - Don't know what?
jokes.setup: Character questions brushing hairless creatures
jokes.punchline: The buggalo don't know they have no hair, followed by them immediately proving they don't understand
jokes.explanation: Clever three-part structure with immediate payoff. The buggalo's instant 'Don't know what?' proves the point perfectly.

jokes.text: O-M-Jah! Don't know I missed them.
jokes.setup: Buggalo responding to emotional reunion
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'Oh my God' as 'O-M-Jah' in broken grammar
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay establishing buggalo speech patterns. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: - Yet. - I don't know how I missed you either.
jokes.setup: Amy says they don't eat Betsy because she's a pet
jokes.punchline: Leo adds 'Yet' implying they will eventually eat the pet
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word completely changes the meaning. Dark humor typical of the show.

jokes.text: No, no, females too wimpy. Buggalo matadors only fight the males.
jokes.setup: Amy worries about her pet Betsy being hurt in fights
jokes.punchline: Leo's casually sexist dismissal that females are 'too wimpy' for the violent sport
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate casual misogyny that works as both character development and commentary

jokes.text: How do I keep missing these things?
jokes.setup: Character consistently missing obvious things happening around them
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment of their obliviousness
jokes.explanation: Running gag setup that will pay off later. Mild humor now but builds character trait.

jokes.text: Big don't mean smart. Zoidberg's big, but he got the intelligence of a brine shrimp.
jokes.setup: Arguing that brain size doesn't equal intelligence
jokes.punchline: Uses Zoidberg as example with oddly specific 'brine shrimp' comparison
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific insult with vivid comparison. 'Brine shrimp' is oddly specific and funny.

jokes.text: Thank you, sir.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg just got insulted about his intelligence
jokes.punchline: Politely thanks the person who insulted him, proving the insult correct
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - maximum comedy with minimal words. His politeness in response to insult is perfectly in character and proves the insult.

jokes.text: Oh, no. A couple of people get killed, not many. Not dangerous at all. People get killed, you say? Four... Five tops. Plus some robots. But mostly people, you say? I'll do it.
jokes.setup: Amy's dad downplays danger of buggalo running
jokes.punchline: Numbers keep escalating while he insists it's not dangerous, and Bender only cares when people (not robots) die
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke with Bender's typical selfish motivation. The contradiction between 'not dangerous' and death count works well.

jokes.text: Four... Five tops. Plus some robots. But mostly people, you say? I'll do it.
jokes.setup: Leo mentions death toll, including robots
jokes.punchline: Bender's immediate enthusiasm when assured it's mostly people who die
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - he's fine with danger as long as humans suffer more than robots

jokes.text: Uh... How about we let this be your issue?
jokes.setup: Asked if she'll participate in dangerous buggalo running
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly deflects with diplomatic non-answer
jokes.explanation: Realistic awkward deflection. Leela trying to avoid conflict while not taking a stand.

jokes.text: Hello, splatter fans, Don Cunningham here. The Red Planet will run red today, with the blood of its bravest heroes. Also, their guts.
jokes.setup: Sports announcer describing the buggalo running
jokes.punchline: 'Red Planet will run red' wordplay, then casually adds 'Also, their guts'
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay with Mars being red. The casual 'Also, their guts' adds dark humor.

jokes.text: Whew. Ah! Next time, watch where you're going.
jokes.setup: Fry presumably gets trampled or knocked around during the running
jokes.punchline: Tells the buggalo to watch where they're going, as if it's their fault
jokes.explanation: Typical Fry obliviousness - blaming the animals in a running where humans are supposed to get out of the way.

jokes.text: Man! I'm running out of steam. Good thing that's not what I run on.
jokes.setup: Common expression 'running out of steam' during a run
jokes.punchline: Bender clarifies he doesn't literally run on steam
jokes.explanation: Good literal interpretation of idiom. Works because Bender is a robot who would run on something specific.

jokes.text: I'm Bender, baby. What's your name? Marquita Maria Christina Chiquita Alana Paloma Ramona Rosita... - Uh-huh. Right. ...Catalina... - Okay. Lupe Lunes Martes Miercoles Jueves Viernes Sabado Domingo.
jokes.setup: Bender asks for her name
jokes.punchline: Extremely long Spanish name that includes days of the week, with Bender's patience wearing thin
jokes.explanation: Classic escalation joke. The days of the week in the name is absurd, and Bender's reactions sell it.

jokes.text: Cat's got some moves. He's very light on his feet for a two-ton robot.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Bender's dancing/movement
jokes.punchline: 'Light on his feet' immediately contradicted by 'two-ton robot'
jokes.explanation: Simple contradiction joke. The juxtaposition of 'light on his feet' and 'two-ton' works well.

jokes.text: Benihana Bonita Nereida Guadalupe Alvarez.
jokes.setup: Continuation of the impossibly long name
jokes.punchline: Includes 'Benihana' (the Japanese steakhouse chain) in her Spanish name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd detail - putting a Japanese restaurant chain in the middle of traditional Spanish names. Unexpected and memorable.

jokes.text: No! Where'd she go? Oh! I'll never see the love of my life again. And even more tragic, she'll never see me again!
jokes.setup: Bender lamenting losing Marquita
jokes.punchline: Makes it about himself - her not seeing him is 'even more tragic' than his loss
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender narcissism. Starts sympathetic then reveals his true self-centered nature.

jokes.text: What happened to the old matador? He retired. I don't know. It seems dangerous.
jokes.setup: Bender asks about previous matador
jokes.punchline: 'Retired' clearly means died, delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Classic dark euphemism. 'Retired' in context obviously means killed, delivered with perfect deadpan.

jokes.text: Marquita Maria Christina et cetera? I would die a thousand deaths to spend one minute with her.
jokes.setup: Commissioner mentions Marquita as the trainer
jokes.punchline: Bender gives up on her impossibly long name with 'et cetera', then makes dramatic romantic declaration
jokes.explanation: Good callback and character moment. 'Et cetera' is a practical surrender to the name length.

jokes.text: Then you have chosen the right line of work.
jokes.setup: Bender says he'd die a thousand deaths for one minute with her
jokes.punchline: Commissioner notes that matador work will likely fulfill that wish
jokes.explanation: Perfect dry delivery. Takes Bender's romantic hyperbole and makes it literal given the job's danger.

jokes.text: I've never seen so much honor, folks. I just hope Marquita doesn't slip on all that honor.
jokes.setup: Announcer commenting on 'honorable' bullfighting
jokes.punchline: Treats 'honor' as if it's a physical substance you could slip on
jokes.explanation: Clever literalization of abstract concept. Making honor into something physical you could slip on is absurd and funny.

jokes.text: I gotta go. The NFL's up for sale on Craigslist.
jokes.setup: Commissioner needs to leave suddenly
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of the NFL being sold on Craigslist like a used car
jokes.explanation: Great absurd throwaway line. NFL on Craigslist is perfectly ridiculous and delivered casually.

jokes.text: I pay close attention when things are about to get jiggy.
jokes.setup: Marquita impressed he remembers her long name
jokes.punchline: Bender's motivation is entirely sexual
jokes.explanation: Pure Bender - reduces romantic gesture to sexual motivation. In character but predictable.

jokes.text: First study, then jiggy? Patience is the hallmark of the matador.
jokes.setup: Marquita explains they must train first before romance
jokes.punchline: Bender reduces it to 'first study, then jiggy' - treating sex like a reward system
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy. Bender's reductive summary contrasts with Marquita's noble philosophy.

jokes.text: Stabbing? Screaming? Jigging? Clothing.
jokes.setup: Bender guesses violent elements of bullfighting
jokes.punchline: The most important element is simply 'Clothing'
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from violent expectations to mundane reality. 'Jigging' is a funny addition to the list.

jokes.text: So you're experienced? My father died yesterday. You're my first customer.
jokes.setup: Bender asks about the tailor's experience after mention of seven generations
jokes.punchline: Father just died, this is his first day on the job
jokes.explanation: Dark humor twist. Sets up family tradition then reveals complete inexperience.

jokes.text: My father died yesterday. You're my first customer.
jokes.setup: Bender asks if the tailor is experienced
jokes.punchline: Reveals he just inherited the business and has no experience
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect timing - reveals both tragedy and incompetence in one line

jokes.text: Honestly, most of our matador work is repairs. Lots and lots of repairs.
jokes.setup: Discussion about matador outfits
jokes.punchline: Implies most work is fixing clothes after matadors get gored
jokes.explanation: Good dark implication. 'Lots and lots of repairs' suggests frequent goring incidents.

jokes.text: No, I'm not. - This is Beyond Buggalo. - Yuck. What's it made from, plant? No. Pork. I never did like pigs.
jokes.setup: Leela says she's not eating buggalo, reveals it's Beyond Buggalo (fake meat)
jokes.punchline: Instead of being plant-based like expected, it's made from pork, which Amy finds more acceptable than plants
jokes.explanation: Clever parody of Beyond Meat. Subverts expectations twice - fake meat that's real meat, and Amy preferring meat to plants.

jokes.text: I never did like pigs.
jokes.setup: Learning the fake buggalo is made from pork
jokes.punchline: Amy's casual dismissal of pigs while defending buggalo
jokes.explanation: Good character hypocrisy - showing arbitrary nature of her animal advocacy

jokes.text: My word! Amy is absolutely 100% wrong.
jokes.setup: Professor examines buggalo brain with X-ray specs after Amy defends their intelligence
jokes.punchline: Dramatic buildup to completely dismiss Amy's theory
jokes.explanation: Good fake-out structure. Professor's dramatic tone makes the dismissal funnier.

jokes.text: That's the stupidest kind of intelligence.
jokes.setup: Amy mentions buggalo have emotional intelligence
jokes.punchline: Dismisses emotional intelligence as inherently stupid
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective dismissal. The contradiction of 'stupidest kind of intelligence' works well.

jokes.text: I love you, Bender. Shut up, señorita. I know it. Marry me, Bender. Then divorce me. Man, this is my kind of sport.
jokes.setup: Female fan declares love for Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender's dismissive response, fan escalates to marriage then divorce, Bender loves the attention
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Bender's typical ego response. 'Then divorce me' is an unexpected addition.

jokes.text: Marry me, Bender. Then divorce me. Man, this is my kind of sport.
jokes.setup: Proposal followed by immediate divorce request
jokes.punchline: Bender approves of the commitment-free arrangement
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Bender loving the idea of marriage with built-in divorce

jokes.text: Always smooth your compression socks.
jokes.setup: Marquita reminds him of her training
jokes.punchline: Bender remembers something completely unrelated and mundane
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor showing Bender's poor priorities and attention span

jokes.text: Oh, no! My cerveza hat.
jokes.setup: Fry is in danger from the buggalo
jokes.punchline: His concern is for his beer hat rather than his safety
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry priorities - worried about beer hat while in mortal danger. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: It's okay. Five-second rule.
jokes.setup: Fry has presumably been trampled or is in some dangerous situation
jokes.punchline: Applies five-second rule as if he's food that fell on the floor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic. Applying five-second rule to being trampled is perfectly Fry-level stupidity.

jokes.text: They're throwing their shiny metal asses at me?
jokes.setup: Announcer says fembots are throwing their 'dainties' at Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender interprets 'dainties' literally as their metal rear ends
jokes.explanation: Good literal interpretation joke. Bender taking 'dainties' to mean robot body parts is funny and in character.

jokes.text: As a matador, who may or may not still be alive, I know nothing's more annoying than a bugfight getting interrupted by a commercial.
jokes.setup: Commercial break during tense moment
jokes.punchline: Bender acknowledges he might be dead and complains about commercial interruption
jokes.explanation: Clever meta joke breaking fourth wall. Bender's uncertainty about being alive adds to the humor.

jokes.text: The same old shows for an exciting new price.
jokes.setup: Fake ad for Fulu Premium
jokes.punchline: Honest tagline about streaming services raising prices without adding content
jokes.explanation: Sharp commentary on streaming services. 'Exciting new price' for same content is perfectly cynical.

jokes.text: I'm dead if I can't magically pull a sword out of my ass. Hey, wait!
jokes.setup: Bender realizes he needs a weapon desperately
jokes.punchline: Uses crude expression then realizes he might actually have one stored there
jokes.explanation: Great setup/payoff. Takes common crude expression and makes it literal for a robot character.

jokes.text: In lieu of financial remuneration.
jokes.setup: Commissioner awards Bender trophy parts
jokes.punchline: Fancy way of saying 'instead of paying you money'
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about not getting paid. The formal language for 'no money' is appropriately bureaucratic.

jokes.text: Of all the students I have ever taught, you are the one who is alive.
jokes.setup: Marquita praising Bender's performance
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment - his only distinction is surviving
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect economy. Sounds like praise but reveals all other students died.

jokes.text: 'Hay' is for horses. Go on.
jokes.setup: Bender says 'Hey Marquita'
jokes.punchline: Corrects his pronunciation with old-fashioned saying
jokes.explanation: Old-fashioned dad joke. Shows Marquita's formal personality but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: Guilt? - Maybe. I always wondered what guilt felt like.
jokes.setup: Amy suggests Bender is feeling guilt
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual admission that guilt is a new emotion for him
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Bender's usual amorality making guilt a novelty

jokes.text: I'm sorry I killed your friend or uncle. Or fruncle.
jokes.setup: Bender apologizing to buggalo for killing one of them
jokes.punchline: Makes up word 'fruncle' (friend + uncle)
jokes.explanation: Good made-up word that sounds plausible. Shows Bender trying to be thorough in his apology.

jokes.text: What? Who's there? Is that you, Fruncle Friender?
jokes.setup: Bender woken up by buggalo voice
jokes.punchline: Uses 'fruncle' again plus makes up 'Friender' as a name
jokes.explanation: Good callback plus new wordplay. 'Fruncle Friender' has nice alliteration.

jokes.text: Please make yourself comfortable in the place of honor, at the bottom of the sleeping pile.
jokes.setup: Buggalo offering Bender place of honor
jokes.punchline: The 'honor' is being crushed at the bottom of a pile
jokes.explanation: Good ironic 'honor'. The contrast between respect and being crushed works well.

jokes.text: Hang on. I can't breathe. Oh, right. Robots don't breathe. Actually, this is pretty cozy.
jokes.setup: Bender panicking about breathing while crushed
jokes.punchline: Remembers he doesn't need to breathe, then enjoys it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment. Bender's realization about not breathing is perfect robot logic.

jokes.text: All of them! Buggalo can be singular or plural.
jokes.setup: Asked which buggalo spoke to him
jokes.punchline: Makes grammar joke about the word being both singular and plural
jokes.explanation: Mild grammar humor. More clever than funny but shows attention to language detail.

jokes.text: They do not don't! Tell her, Amy.
jokes.setup: Arguing that buggalo can speak
jokes.punchline: Uses double negative 'do not don't' in his excitement
jokes.explanation: Shows Bender's excitement through grammar breakdown. Double negative is realistically flustered speech.

jokes.text: I don't usually do the right thing, but I'll be ruining everyone's day, so it balances out.
jokes.setup: Bender decides to stop the bullfighting
jokes.punchline: Justifies good deed by focusing on the negative consequences for others
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - can only do good by focusing on the harm it causes others. Excellent character consistency.

jokes.text: Look how his legs barely move, as he glides effortlessly with all the majesty and grace of a dog on a skateboard.
jokes.setup: Announcer describing El Gran Buggalo's movement
jokes.punchline: Compares majestic movement to undignified 'dog on a skateboard'
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual simile. Sets up majesty then deflates it with ridiculous comparison.

jokes.text: This is Don 'Beat the Traffic' Cunningham, signing off.
jokes.setup: Announcer ending broadcast during climactic moment
jokes.punchline: Uses nickname 'Beat the Traffic' showing he leaves events early
jokes.explanation: Good character detail. Announcer leaving during the climax for traffic reasons is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: How did you get that off so easily?
jokes.setup: Bender's disguise head comes off easily after he struggled
jokes.punchline: Complains about the inconsistency rather than focusing on being stabbed
jokes.explanation: Good priority joke. Bender focuses on the technical inconsistency while ignoring mortal danger.

jokes.text: Luckily, the sword just went through my head.
jokes.setup: Bender reassuring about his injury
jokes.punchline: Being stabbed through the head is the lucky outcome
jokes.explanation: Good robot anatomy humor - head injury being minor due to mechanical nature

jokes.text: Luckily, the sword just went through my head.
jokes.setup: Bender was stabbed through the head
jokes.punchline: His casual dismissal because robots can survive head wounds
jokes.explanation: Good use of robot physiology to make a potentially fatal wound into casual inconvenience

jokes.text: He went home!
jokes.setup: Bender addresses Don Cunningham in his speech
jokes.punchline: Realizes the announcer already left to beat traffic
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier joke. Interrupts serious speech with practical observation.

jokes.text: Yet noble...
jokes.setup: Bender calls bullfighting barbaric yet noble yet barbaric
jokes.punchline: Crowd only responds to 'yet noble' part, ignoring the criticism
jokes.explanation: Good selective hearing joke. Crowd only hears what supports their position.

jokes.text: But gambling is still legal. Nothing wrong with gambling. I brought mines with me.
jokes.setup: Bullfighting is banned but gambling is still legal
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals he brought gambling materials, showing his priorities
jokes.explanation: Shows character priorities. Quick pivot from banned activity to still-legal vice.

jokes.text: My word! It seems that while each individual buggalo is brainless, their empty skulls form resonance chambers that allow large groups to assemble a hive mind.
jokes.setup: Buggalo reveal they can talk
jokes.punchline: Scientific explanation that their brains are literally empty but work as echo chambers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pseudo-science explanation. Empty skulls as resonance chambers is creative and funny.

jokes.text: Much of our culture is built around this ancient tradition. It's not a very rich culture, we admit that. We just like to eat grass and kill matadors.
jokes.setup: Buggalo defending their bullfighting tradition
jokes.punchline: Admits their culture isn't rich and reduces it to eating and killing
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan honesty. Self-aware about cultural limitations while defending violent tradition.

jokes.text: Much of our culture is built around this ancient tradition. It's not a very rich culture, we admit that.
jokes.setup: Buggalo defending their fighting tradition
jokes.punchline: Their honest admission about their culture's limitations
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about cultural limitations while defending tradition

jokes.text: It was actually only part of a night. And I didn't do much sleeping.
jokes.setup: Explaining his night with the buggalo
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was having sex with multiple buggalo all night
jokes.explanation: Pure Bender behavior. Explains the multiple romantic entanglements with typical directness.

jokes.text: It was actually only part of a night. And I didn't do much sleeping.
jokes.setup: Explaining his 'whole night sleeping with the buggalo'
jokes.punchline: Clarification that makes it sound even more scandalous
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes the innocent explanation sound worse through implication
